label: inference
headline_statement: Theorem 2 (\texttt{inference}) is confirmed: under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:regular}, \ref{ass:stable-dual}, and \ref{ass:finite-cell}, define \(\widehat L_\rho=\sum_{j=1}^J\widehat p_j\min_{\pi\in\Pi_{K_n}(a_-,a_+)}\sum_{\ell,m}\one\{\widehat Q_1(u_\ell\mid x_j)>\widehat Q_0(u_m\mid x_j)\}\pi_{\ell m}\) and \(\widehat U_\rho=\sum_{j=1}^J\widehat p_j\max_{\pi\in\Pi_{K_n}(a_-,a_+)}\sum_{\ell,m}\one\{\widehat Q_1(u_\ell\mid x_j)>\widehat Q_0(u_m\mid x_j)\}\pi_{\ell m}\), where \(\Pi_{K_n}(a_-,a_+)=\{\pi\in\mathbb R_+^{K_n\times K_n}:\sum_m\pi_{\ell m}=K_n^{-1},\sum_\ell\pi_{\ell m}=K_n^{-1},a_-\le\sum_{\ell,m}u_\ell u_m\pi_{\ell m}\le a_+\}\) and \(a_\pm=(\rho_\pm+3)/12\). Let \(O_1^*,\ldots,O_{m_n}^*\) be drawn with replacement from the empirical law and let \(\widehat L_\rho^*,\widehat U_\rho^*\) be the same two programs recomputed on the bootstrap sample with the same grid \(K_n\). If \(b_{K_n}=o(n^{-1/2})\), the root-\(n\) local sieve remainder clause holds, meaning with \(e_K(\eta)=\Phi_{\rho,K}(\eta)-\Phi_\rho(\eta)\), \(\eta_0=\eta(P)\), and \(\widehat\eta_n\) the empirical finite-cell summary, \(\sqrt n\{e_{K_n}(\widehat\eta_n)-e_{K_n}(\eta_0)\}=o_p(1)\), \(\log K_n/n\to0\), and \(m_n\to\infty\) with \(m_n/n\to0\), then \(Z_n=\sqrt n(\widehat L_\rho-L_\rho(P),\widehat U_\rho-U_\rho(P))\rightsquigarrow\dot\Phi_{\rho,P}(\mathbb G_P)\) and \(d_{\mathrm{BL}}(\mathcal L^*(Z_n^*\mid O_1,\ldots,O_n),\mathcal L\{\dot\Phi_{\rho,P}(\mathbb G_P)\})\to_p0\) for \(Z_n^*=\sqrt{m_n}(\widehat L_\rho^*-\widehat L_\rho,\widehat U_\rho^*-\widehat U_\rho)\), where \(\mathbb G_P\) is the joint finite-cell empirical-weight and conditional-quantile Gaussian limit and \(\dot\Phi_{\rho,P}\) is the directional derivative of \(\Phi_\rho(\eta)=(L_\rho(\eta),U_\rho(\eta))\) at \(\eta(P)\).
review_repair_notes: The patched proof no longer assumes a shifted directional expansion. It subtracts two ordinary population-base directional expansions, displays the drift term A_n^*, and proves A_n^*=o_p^*(1) from conditional tightness of H_n^*, R_n=o_p(1), and continuity of the directional derivative on the stable face.
